south||{{ill|West-Southwest|de|West-Southwest|ja|方位|lt=西南西|lt_ja=西南西|lt_de=West-Southwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West-Southwest|qq=}} slightly west |- |{{ill|Hei|de|Hei|ja|丙|lt=丙|lt_ja=丙|lt_de=Hei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei|qq=}}・{{ill|Shin|de|Shin|ja|辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|lt=丙|lt_ja=丙|lt_de=Hei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei|qq=}}・{{ill|Shin|de|Shin|ja|辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}
{{ill|Bo|de|Bo|ja|戊|lt=戊|lt_ja=戊|lt_de=Bo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bo|qq=}}・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|癸|lt=癸|lt_ja=癸|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||1・6
3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|丁|" to "|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|"
revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" !colspan="2"|Year!!colspan="6"|Ehou |- !{{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}}!!Last digit of AD year!!{{ill|24 {{ill|Tiger|de|Tiger|ja|寅|lt=Tiger|lt_ja=寅|lt_de=Tiger|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger|qq=}} and {{ill|Rabbit|de|Kaninchen|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie north||{{ill|East-Northeast|de|Ost-Nordost|ja|方位|lt=東北東|lt_ja=東北東|lt_de=East-Northeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East-Northeast|qq=}} slightly east |- {{ill|Monkey|de|Affe|ja|申|lt=Monkey|lt_ja=申|lt_de=Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monkey|qq=}} and {{ill|Rooster|de|Hahn|ja|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|lt=Rooster|lt_ja=酉|lt_de=Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}||{{0}}8:30||255°||{{ill|West by South|de|West by South|ja|方位|lt=西微南|lt_ja=西微南|lt_de=West by South|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West by South|qq=}} slightly south||{{ill|West-Southwest|de|West-Southwest|ja|方位|lt=西南西|lt_ja=西南西|lt_de=West-Southwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West-Southwest|qq=}} slightly west |- (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|lt=丙|lt_ja=丙|lt_de=Hei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei|qq=}}・{{ill|Shin|de|Shin|ja|辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}
{{ill|Bo|de|Bo|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=戊|lt_ja=戊|lt_de=Bo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bo|qq=}}・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|癸|lt=癸|lt_ja=癸|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||1・6
3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|lt=Rooster|lt_ja=酉|lt_de=Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}||{{0}}8:30||255°||{{ill|West by South|de|West by South|ja|方位|lt=西微南|lt_ja=西微南|lt_de=West by South|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West by South|qq=}} slightly south||{{ill|West-Southwest|de|West-Southwest|ja|方位|lt=西南西|lt_ja=西南西|lt_de=West-Southwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West-Southwest|qq=}} slightly west |- |{{ill|Hei|de|Hei|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|lt=丙|lt_ja=丙|lt_de=Hei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei|qq=}}・{{ill|Shin|de|Shin|ja|辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}
{{ill|Bo|de|Bo|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=戊|lt_ja=戊|lt_de=Bo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bo|qq=}}・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|癸|lt=癸|lt_ja=癸|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||1・6
3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" !colspan="2"|Year!!colspan="6"|Ehou |- !{{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}}!!Last digit of AD year!!{{ill|24 Directions|de|24 Richtungen|ja|二十四山|lt=24 Directions|lt_ja=二十四山|lt_de=24 Richtungen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24 Directions|qq=}}!!12 Zodiac Animals!!{{ill|Clock Position|de|Uhrzeigersinn|ja|クロックポジション|lt=Clock Method|lt_ja=時計法|lt_de=Clock Method|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clock Position|qq=}}!!{{ill|Azimuth|de|Azimut|ja|方位角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azimuth|qq=}}!!{{ill|Compass Orientation|de|Kompassrichtung|ja|羅針方位|lt=32 Directions|lt_ja=32方位|lt_de=32 Directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Compass Orientation|qq=}}!!16 Directions |- |{{ill|Kou|de|Kou|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|lt=甲|lt_ja=甲|lt_de=Kou|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kou|qq=}}・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|lt=己|lt_ja=己|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||4・9||Kou||Between {{ill|Tiger|de|Tiger|ja|寅|lt=Tiger|lt_ja=寅|lt_de=Tiger|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger|qq=}} and {{ill|Rabbit|de|Kaninchen|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|lt=Rabbit|lt_ja=卯|lt_de=Kaninchen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}}||{{0}}2:30||{{0}}75°||{{ill|East by North|de|Ost bei Nord|ja|方位|lt=東微北|lt_ja=東微北|lt_de=East by North|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East by North|qq=}} slightly north||{{ill|East-Northeast|de|Ost-Nordost|ja|方位|lt=東北東|lt_ja=東北東|lt_de=East-Northeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East-Northeast|qq=}} slightly east |- |{{ill|Otsu|de|Otsu|ja|乙|1=Kinoto (乙)|lt=Kinoto (乙)|2=ja|3=乙|4=zh|5=乙|lt=乙|lt_ja=乙|lt_de=Otsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsu|qq=}}・{{ill|Kō|de|Kō|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|lt=Kanoe (庚)|2=ja|3=庚|4=zh|5=庚|lt=庚|lt_ja=庚|lt_de=Kō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|qq=}}||0・5||Kō||Between {{ill|Monkey|de|Affe|ja|申|lt=Monkey|lt_ja=申|lt_de=Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monkey|qq=}} and {{ill|Rooster|de|Hahn|ja|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|lt=Rooster|lt_ja=酉|lt_de=Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}||{{0}}8:30||255°||{{ill|West by South|de|West by South|ja|方位|lt=西微南|lt_ja=西微南|lt_de=West by South|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West by South|qq=}} slightly south||{{ill|West-Southwest|de|West-Southwest|ja|方位|lt=西南西|lt_ja=西南西|lt_de=West-Southwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West-Southwest|qq=}} slightly west |- |{{ill|Hei|de|Hei|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|lt=丙|lt_ja=丙|lt_de=Hei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei|qq=}}・{{ill|Shin|de|Shin|ja|辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}

3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}

3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}

3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" !colspan="2"|Year!!colspan="6"|Ehou |- !{{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}}!!Last digit of AD year!!{{ill|24 Directions|de|24 Richtungen|ja|二十四山|lt=24 Directions|lt_ja=二十四山|lt_de=24 Richtungen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24 Directions|qq=}}!!12 Zodiac Animals!!{{ill|Clock Position|de|Uhrzeigersinn|ja|クロックポジション|lt=Clock Method|lt_ja=時計法|lt_de=Clock Method|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clock Position|qq=}}!!{{ill|Azimuth|de|Azimut|ja|方位角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azimuth|qq=}}!!{{ill|Compass Orientation|de|Kompassrichtung|ja|羅針方位|lt=32 Directions|lt_ja=32方位|lt_de=32 Directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Compass Orientation|qq=}}!!16 Directions |- |{{ill|Kou|de|Kou|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|lt=甲|lt_ja=甲|lt_de=Kou|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kou|qq=}}・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|lt=己|lt_ja=己|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||4・9||Kou||Between {{ill|Tiger|de|Tiger|ja|寅|lt=Tiger|lt_ja=寅|lt_de=Tiger|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger|qq=}} and {{ill|Rabbit|de|Kaninchen|ja|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|lt=Rabbit|lt_ja=卯|lt_de=Kaninchen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}}||{{0}}2:30||{{0}}75°||{{ill|East by North|de|Ost bei Nord|ja|方位|lt=東微北|lt_ja=東微北|lt_de=East by North|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East by North|qq=}} slightly north||{{ill|East-Northeast|de|Ost-Nordost|ja|方位|lt=東北東|lt_ja=東北東|lt_de=East-Northeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East-Northeast|qq=}} slightly east |- |{{ill|Otsu|de|Otsu|ja|乙|1=Kinoto (乙)|lt=Kinoto (乙)|2=ja|3=乙|4=zh|5=乙|lt=乙|lt_ja=乙|lt_de=Otsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsu|qq=}}・{{ill|Kō|de|Kō|ja|庚|1=Kanoe (庚)|lt=Kanoe (庚)|2=ja|3=庚|4=zh|5=庚|lt=庚|lt_ja=庚|lt_de=Kō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|qq=}}||0・5||Kō||Between {{ill|Monkey|de|Affe|ja|申|lt=Monkey|lt_ja=申|lt_de=Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monkey|qq=}} and {{ill|Rooster|de|Hahn|ja|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|lt=Rooster|lt_ja=酉|lt_de=Rooster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}||{{0}}8:30||255°||{{ill|West by South|de|West by South|ja|方位|lt=西微南|lt_ja=西微南|lt_de=West by South|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West by South|qq=}} slightly south||{{ill|West-Southwest|de|West-Southwest|ja|方位|lt=西南西|lt_ja=西南西|lt_de=West-Southwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West-Southwest|qq=}} slightly west |- |{{ill|Hei|de|Hei|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|lt=丙|lt_ja=丙|lt_de=Hei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei|qq=}}・{{ill|Shin|de|Shin|ja|辛|1=Kanoto (辛)|lt=Kanoto (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}

3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? 3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025?
