（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? 3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|1=Horse (Zodiac)|lt=Horse (午)|2=ja|3=午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||{{ill|South by East|de|Süd bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" （'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}

bei Ost|ja|方位|lt=南微東|lt_ja=南微東|lt_de=South by East|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South by East|qq=}} slightly east||{{ill|South-Southeast|de|Süd-Südost|ja|方位|lt=南南東|lt_ja=南南東|lt_de=South-Southeast|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South-Southeast|qq=}} slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|1=Pig (Zodiac)|lt=Pig (亥)|2=ja|3=亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:New Year's Seasonal Words]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|1=Cardinal direction|lt=Five directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" !colspan="2"|Year!!colspan="6"|Ehou |- !{{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}}!!Last digit of AD year!!{{ill|24 Directions|de|24 Richtungen|ja|二十四山|lt=24 Directions|lt_ja=二十四山|lt_de=24 Richtungen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24 Directions|qq=}}!!12 Zodiac Animals!!{{ill|Clock Position|de|Uhrzeigersinn|ja|クロックポジション|lt=Clock Method|lt_ja=時計法|lt_de=Clock Method|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clock Position|qq=}}!!{{ill|Azimuth|de|Azimut|ja|方位角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azimuth|qq=}}!!{{ill|Compass Orientation|de|Kompassrichtung|ja|羅針方位|lt=32 Directions|lt_ja=32方位|lt_de=32 Directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Compass Orientation|qq=}}!!16 Directions |- |{{ill|Kou|de|Kou|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|lt=甲|lt_ja=甲|lt_de=Kou|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kou|qq=}}・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|1=Cardinal direction|lt=Five directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Orientations]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|1=Cardinal direction|lt=Five directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between {{ill|Boar|de|Schwein|ja|亥|1=Pig (Zodiac)|lt=Pig (亥)|2=ja|3=亥|lt=Boar|lt_ja=亥|lt_de=Boar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boar|qq=}} and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||{{ill|North by West|de|Nord bei West|ja|方位|1=Cardinal direction|lt=Five directions|lt=北微西|lt_ja=北微西|lt_de=North by West|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North by West|qq=}} slightly west||{{ill|North-Northwest|de|Nord-Nordwest|ja|方位|1=Cardinal direction|lt=Five directions|lt=北北西|lt_ja=北北西|lt_de=North-Northwest|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North-Northwest|qq=}} slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ({{ill|Hatsumode|de|Hatsumode|ja|初詣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsumode|qq=}}) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Setsubun|de|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * {{ill|Feng Shui|de|Feng Shui|ja|風水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feng Shui|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakahi-no-Hime|de|Wakahi-no-Hime|ja|稚日女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakahi-no-Hime|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Taoist Deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in {{ill|Onmyōdō|de|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} who governs the blessings of the {{ill|year|de|Jahr|ja|年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/year|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', {{ill|Year Deity|de|Jahresgott|ja|年神|lt=歳神|lt_ja=歳神|lt_de=Jahresgott|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year Deity|qq=}}, and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most {{ill|calendar|de|Kalender|ja|暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar|qq=}}s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of {{ill|Ox-Headed Heavenly King|de|Ochsenkopf-Himmelskönig|ja|牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox-Headed Heavenly King|qq=}} and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=須佐之男尊|lt_ja=須佐之男尊|lt_de=Susanoo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Syncretism|de|Synkretismus|ja|習合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Syncretism|qq=}}, he is also identified with his consort, {{ill|Kushinadahime|de|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=櫛稲田姫|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫|lt_de=Kushinadahime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}. == Ehou == The {{ill|orientation|de|Ausrichtung|ja|方位|1=Cardinal direction|lt=Five directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/orientation|qq=}} in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, {{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|de|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 33) {{ill|May 23|de|23. Mai|ja|5月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 23|qq=}} first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}} as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" !colspan="2"|Year!!colspan="6"|Ehou |- !{{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|de|Zehn Himmelsstämme|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten Heavenly Stems|qq=}}!!Last digit of AD year!!{{ill|24 Directions|de|24 Richtungen|ja|二十四山|lt=24 Directions|lt_ja=二十四山|lt_de=24 Richtungen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24 Directions|qq=}}!!12 Zodiac Animals!!{{ill|Clock Position|de|Uhrzeigersinn|ja|クロックポジション|lt=Clock Method|lt_ja=時計法|lt_de=Clock Method|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clock Position|qq=}}!!{{ill|Azimuth|de|Azimut|ja|方位角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azimuth|qq=}}!!{{ill|Compass Orientation|de|Kompassrichtung|ja|羅針方位|lt=32 Directions|lt_ja=32方位|lt_de=32 Directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Compass Orientation|qq=}}!!16 Directions |- |{{ill|Kou|de|Kou|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] who governs the blessings of the [[:en:Year|year]]. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', [[:en:Toshigami|歳神]], and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most [[:en:Calendar|calendar]]s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Ox-Headed Heavenly King]] and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|須佐之男尊]] and [[:en:Religious syncretism|Syncretism]], he is also identified with his consort, [[:en:Kushinadahime|櫛稲田姫]]. == Ehou == The [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]] in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, [[:en:1958|1958]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 33) [[:en:May 23|May 23]] first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" (辛)|2=ja|3=辛|4=zh|5=辛|lt=辛|lt_ja=辛|lt_de=Shin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin|qq=}}
{{ill|Bo|de|Bo|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|lt=戊|lt_ja=戊|lt_de=Bo|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bo|qq=}}・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|lt=癸|lt_ja=癸|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||1・6
3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|1=Horse (Zodiac)|lt=Horse (午)|2=ja|3=午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南微東]] slightly east||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南南東]] slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北微西]] slightly west||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北北西]] slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ([[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumode]]) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] * [[:en:Feng shui|Feng Shui]] * [[:en:Wakahiru-me|Wakahi-no-Hime]] {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] who governs the blessings of the [[:en:Year|year]]. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', [[:en:Toshigami|歳神]], and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most [[:en:Calendar|calendar]]s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Ox-Headed Heavenly King]] and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|須佐之男尊]] and [[:en:Religious syncretism|Syncretism]], he is also identified with his consort, [[:en:Kushinadahime|櫛稲田姫]]. == Ehou == The [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]] in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, [[:en:1958|1958]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 33) [[:en:May 23|May 23]] first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? 3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|1=Horse (Zodiac)|lt=Horse (午)|2=ja|3=午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南微東]] slightly east||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南南東]] slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北微西]] slightly west||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北北西]] slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ([[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumode]]) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] * [[:en:Feng shui|Feng Shui]] * [[:en:Wakahiru-me|Wakahi-no-Hime]] {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:新春の季語]] [[Category:方位]]

revision.text:
[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] who governs the blessings of the [[:en:Year|year]]. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', [[:en:Toshigami|歳神]], and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most [[:en:Calendar|calendar]]s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Ox-Headed Heavenly King]] and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|須佐之男尊]] and [[:en:Religious syncretism|Syncretism]], he is also identified with his consort, [[:en:Kushinadahime|櫛稲田姫]]. == Ehou == The [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]] in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, [[:en:1958|1958]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 33) [[:en:May 23|May 23]] first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" !colspan="2"|Year!!colspan="6"|Ehou |- ![[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]]!!Last digit of AD year!!{{ill|24 Directions|de|24 Richtungen|ja|二十四山|lt=24 Directions|lt_ja=二十四山|lt_de=24 Richtungen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24 Directions|qq=}}!!12 Zodiac Animals!![[:en:Clock position|Clock Method]]!![[:en:Azimuth|Azimuth]]!!{{ill|Compass (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|lt=Rabbit|lt_ja=卯|lt_de=Kaninchen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}}||{{0}}2:30||{{0}}75°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|東微北]] slightly north||[[:en:Cardinal direction|東北東]] slightly east |- |{{ill|Otsu|de|Otsu|ja|乙|1=Kinoto (乙)|lt=Kinoto 3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|1=Horse (Zodiac)|lt=Horse (午)|2=ja|3=午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南微東]] slightly east||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南南東]] slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北微西]] slightly west||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北北西]] slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ([[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumode]]) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. 3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|1=Horse (Zodiac)|lt=Horse (午)|2=ja|3=午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南微東]] slightly east||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南南東]] slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・{{ill|Jin|de|Jin|ja|壬|1=Mizunoe (壬)|lt=Mizunoe (壬)|2=ja|3=壬|4=zh|5=壬|lt=壬|lt_ja=壬|lt_de=Jin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jin|qq=}}||2・7||Jin||Between [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] and {{ill|Rat|de|Ratte|ja|子|1=Rat (zodiac)|lt=Rat (子)|2=ja|3=子 (十二支)|lt=子|lt_ja=子|lt_de=Rat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rat|qq=}}||11:30||345°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北微西]] slightly west||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北北西]] slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ([[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumode]]) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] * [[:en:Feng shui|Feng Shui]] * [[:en:Wakahiru-me|Wakahi-no-Hime]] {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:新春の季語]] [[Category:方位]] [[Category:Japanese folk religion]] [[Category:Taoist deities]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] who governs the blessings of the [[:en:Year|year]]. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', [[:en:Toshigami|歳神]], and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most [[:en:Calendar|calendar]]s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Ox-Headed Heavenly King]] and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|須佐之男尊]] and [[:en:Religious syncretism|Syncretism]], he is also identified with his consort, [[:en:Kushinadahime|櫛稲田姫]]. == Ehou == The [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]] in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, [[:en:1958|1958]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 33) [[:en:May 23|May 23]] first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" Richtungen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24 Directions|qq=}}!!12 Zodiac Animals!![[:en:Clock position|Clock Method]]!![[:en:Azimuth|Azimuth]]!!{{ill|Compass In most [[:en:Calendar|calendar]]s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Ox-Headed Heavenly King]] and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|須佐之男尊]] and [[:en:Religious syncretism|Syncretism]], he is also identified with his consort, [[:en:Kushinadahime|櫛稲田姫]]. == Ehou == The [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]] in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, [[:en:1958|1958]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 33) [[:en:May 23|May 23]] first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025? Learn the origin, rules of eating, and how to attract good fortune!|accessdate=2025-02-12|publisher=AllAbout}}. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;white-space:nowrap;" !colspan="2"|Year!!colspan="6"|Ehou |- ![[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]]!!Last digit of AD year!!{{ill|24 Directions|de|24 Richtungen|ja|二十四山|lt=24 Directions|lt_ja=二十四山|lt_de=24 Richtungen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/24 Directions|qq=}}!!12 Zodiac Animals!![[:en:Clock position|Clock Method]]!![[:en:Azimuth|Azimuth]]!!{{ill|Compass Orientation|de|Kompassrichtung|ja|羅針方位|lt=32 Directions|lt_ja=32方位|lt_de=32 Directions|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Compass Orientation|qq=}}!!16 Directions |- |[[:en:Kou|甲]]・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|lt=己|lt_ja=己|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||4・9||Kou||Between [[:en:Tiger|Tiger]] and [[:en:Rabbit|Rabbit]]||{{0}}2:30||{{0}}75°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|東微北]] slightly north||[[:en:Cardinal direction|東北東]] slightly east |- |{{ill|Otsu|de|Otsu|ja|乙|1=Kinoto (乙)|lt=Kinoto (乙)|2=ja|3=乙|4=zh|5=乙|lt=乙|lt_ja=乙|lt_de=Otsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsu|qq=}}・[[:en:Ko fight|庚]]||0・5||Kō||Between [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey]] and [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster]]||{{0}}8:30||255°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|西微南]] slightly south||[[:en:Cardinal direction|西南西]] slightly west |- |{{ill|Hei|de|Hei|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|lt=丙|lt_ja=丙|lt_de=Hei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hei|qq=}}・[[:en:Shin|辛]]
[[:en:Bo|戊]]・{{ill|Ki|de|Ki|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|lt=癸|lt_ja=癸|lt_de=Ki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki|qq=}}||1・6
3・8||Hei||Between {{ill|Snake|de|Schlange|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|lt=Snake|lt_ja=巳|lt_de=Snake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake|qq=}} and {{ill|Horse|de|Pferd|ja|午|1=Horse (Zodiac)|lt=Horse (午)|2=ja|3=午|lt=Horse|lt_ja=午|lt_de=Horse|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horse|qq=}}||{{0}}5:30||165°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南微東]] slightly east||[[:en:Cardinal direction|南南東]] slightly south |- |{{ill|Tei|de|Tei|ja|丁|1=Hinoto (丁)|lt=Hinoto (丁)|2=ja|3=丁|4=zh|5=丁|lt=丁|lt_ja=丁|lt_de=Tei|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tei|qq=}}・[[:en:Jin|壬]]||2・7||Jin||Between [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] and [[:en:Ratte|子]]||11:30||345°||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北微西]] slightly west||[[:en:Cardinal direction|北北西]] slightly north |} Previously, it was customary to perform the New Year's visit ([[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumode]]) by going from home to a shrine or temple located in the Ehou direction ({{ill|Ehou-mairi|de|Ehou-Besuch|ja|恵方詣り|lt=Ehou-mairi|lt_ja=恵方詣り|lt_de=Ehou-mairi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-mairi|qq=}}). Additionally, as a Setsubun event, there was a custom—primarily in the Kansai region—of facing Ehou and eating an entire thick sushi roll ({{ill|Sushi Roll|de|Sushi Rolle|ja|巻き寿司|lt=Thick Roll|lt_ja=太巻き|lt_de=Thick Roll|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sushi Roll|qq=}}) in one bite, known as the {{ill|Ehou-maki|de|Ehou-maki|ja|恵方巻|lt=Ehou-maki|lt_ja=恵方巻|lt_de=Ehou-maki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehou-maki|qq=}} custom, which is gradually gaining nationwide recognition through promotions and campaigns by convenience stores and others. Note that for such promotions, a simplified explanation based on the common 16 directions is often provided instead of the full 24 directions. == Footnotes == == Related Topics == * [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] * [[:en:Feng shui|Feng Shui]] * [[:en:Wakahiru-me|Wakahi-no-Hime]] {{translated page|ja|歳徳神|version=103714853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onmyōdō Deities]] [[Category:Japanese Folk Beliefs]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Toshitoku-shin]] [[ja:歳徳神]] [[de:Toshitoku Gott]] [[ja:%E6%AD%B3%E5%BE%B3%E7%A5%9E]] [[Category:新春の季語]] [[Category:方位]] [[Category:Japanese folk religion]] [[Category:Taoist deities]] [[Category:Onmyōdō deities]]

[[File:God of Toshitoku.png|thumb|right|150px|'''Toshitoku God'''
（'Abe Seimei's Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation'）]] [[File:Ehou-direction.png|thumb|right|270px|Ehou Direction]] '''Toshitoku God''' (Toshitokujin, Tondosan) is a deity in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] who governs the blessings of the [[:en:Year|year]]. It is also known as '''Nen Toku''', [[:en:Toshigami|歳神]], and '''New Year's Deity'''. In most [[:en:Calendar|calendar]]s, the opening pages include an entry for the beautiful princess deity—resembling a queen—who represents Toshitoku God. There are various theories regarding the origin of Toshitoku God. In ''{{ill|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|de|Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|ja|簠簋内伝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuugi Naiden Illustrated Explanation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集}}'', it is stated that he is the consort of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Ox-Headed Heavenly King]] and that his consort is the mother of the {{ill|Eight General Deities|de|Acht Generalgötter|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight General Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}, namely {{ill|Harisaijo|de|Harisaijo|ja|頗梨采女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisaijo|qq=}} (Harisaijo), although {{誰範囲|date=October 2024|This has been criticized as nonsense}}. Furthermore, because Ox-Headed Heavenly King syncretized with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|須佐之男尊]] and [[:en:Religious syncretism|Syncretism]], he is also identified with his consort, [[:en:Kushinadahime|櫛稲田姫]]. == Ehou == The [[:en:Cardinal direction|Five directions]] in which Toshitoku God resides is called '''Ehou''' (Ehou, Lucky Direction, or Brother Direction), or '''Akino Kata''' (Akino Kata), and it is believed that if one performs activities facing that direction, all undertakings will be auspicious."Nenchū Gyōji Jiten", pp. 103–104, [[:en:1958|1958]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 33) [[:en:May 23|May 23]] first edition published, edited by {{ill|Masataka Nishikado|de|Masataka Nishikado|ja|西角井正慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Nishikado|qq=}}, Tōkyodō Publishing. The orientation (i.e., Ehou) in which Toshitoku God resides is determined by the year's [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]] as shown below{{Cite web|url=https://allabout.co.jp/gm/gc/220582/#3|title=What is the Direction of the Ehou-maki? When is Setsubun 2025?
